questions.question: The Queen's Stepwell in Gujarat, known officially as Rani-ki-Vav, is situated along the course of which river?
The typical landform seen in the Thar Desert is:
Which of the following pairs of neighbouring countries are landlocked and share only land borders with India?
Which of the following represents the longitudinal extent of India?
The Hazira–Vijaipur–Jagdishpur (HVJ) pipeline is mainly used to transport:
Which Indian state shares its border with China, Nepal and Bhutan?
The Earth completes one rotation on its axis approximately in:
Which of the following minerals is known as 'Brown Gold' in India?
The northern part of the plains along the Bay of Bengal is referred to as the Northern Circar, while the southern part is known as the ___________.
The Tehri Dam, which serves multiple purposes including power generation, is built on which river?
In which of the following geographical areas of India is Kumaoni spoken by a significant portion of the population?
Which of the following belongs to the Lakshadweep group of islands?
Which of the following is an impact of river meandering in plains?
Which of the following deserts is visible in the southern part of the world map?
The concept of 'watershed' is primarily associated with:
What is the primary reason for overexploitation of groundwater in Punjab and Haryana?
questions.option_1: Government restrictions on surface water use
questions.option_2: Presence of sandy soils with poor water retention
questions.option_3: Excessive use of water for domestic consumption
questions.option_4: Cultivation of water-intensive crops like paddy

questions.question: The western cyclonic disturbances that enter the Indian subcontinent from the west and the northwest during the winter months originate from which of the following regions?
What is the correct order of arrival of seismic waves at a seismograph after an earthquake?
Which drainage pattern resembles the branches of a tree and is common in rivers of the northern plains?
Which of the following projects is designed to provide irrigation to Rajasthan’s desert areas?
The Western Ghats and the northeastern hills of India are primarily covered with which type of natural vegetation?
In which of the following Indian regions is an alpine climate with snowy winters and cool summers predominant?
Which physical factor most influences the formation of dispersed settlements in hilly and forested regions of India?
Which of the following is not a factor that affects ocean currents?
Which of the following is a distinctive feature of tribal settlements in India?
questions.option_1: Scattered or linear village pattern in forested or hilly areas
